body.title: [awaiting_validation] Honesty gate: load-bearing tickets cannot close without a HEAD-valid proof
body.intention: A ticket classified load-bearing cannot reach status=closed unless it points to a proof whose commit==current HEAD; missing/stale proof blocks the close, while a ticket explicitly marked exploration closes as shipped-unproven with a reason.
body.description:
**Problem:** A proof emitter is necessary but not sufficient — nothing forces a close to point at one. Without this gate, proofs are optional decoration and 'done' stays a free claim.

**Load-bearing discriminator (advisor blocker):** the gate must MECHANICALLY decide load-bearing vs exploration, or it either taxes everything (turtles-to-dust) or relies on builder self-declaration (the very escape hatch the consequence ticket flags). Rule: derive from dependence where possible — a ticket whose Affected files are imported by other non-test code is load-bearing; fall back to an explicit `proof_scope: load_bearing|exploration` field when dependence can't be determined. Default when ambiguous = load_bearing (CP1: assume it matters until shown otherwise).
**shipped-unproven (advisor):** reconcile with the canonical status model (`ticket_status.py` / `effective_status()`) — implement as a FLAG on closed (`proven: false` + reason), NOT a new salience status, so it doesn't collide with the CONSEQUENCE·DEPENDENCY·READY… taxonomy. A closed-but-unproven ticket renders distinctly.
**Commit-binding:** a proof whose `commit` is behind HEAD is stale → close blocked / re-run demanded (free hollow-drift detection).
**Bootstrap exception:** this foundational piece closes on its pytest gate + advisor/inspection, not a self-emitted proof.

**Affected files:** `devlab/claudecode/cc_queue.py` close/done path (MEDIUM inertia — pre-approval surfaced); `unseen_university/ticket_status.py` (shipped-unproven flag rendering); proof-lookup helper (reads emission store).
**Design rules:** filesystem-memory-store; prove-what's-load-bearing (the scoping rule); ticket-status-model (don't break effective_status taxonomy); no-sqlite.
**Scope boundary:** IN — load-bearing discriminator, close-path proof check, HEAD-vs-proof-commit staleness, shipped-unproven flag. OUT — the emitter (gated dep), sufficiency judgement (Evaluator ticket), the atomicity SPLIT check.
**Test plan:** close load-bearing ticket w/ no proof → rejected; w/ stale-commit proof → rejected; w/ HEAD-valid proof → allowed. Close an exploration-classified ticket with shipped-unproven+reason → allowed, renders as closed-unproven. Assert dependence-derived classification matches expectation on a fixture imported elsewhere vs a standalone script.
**Completion criteria:** the five close attempts above behave as specified in an automated test; effective_status() renders a shipped-unproven close distinctly from a proven close; a closed load-bearing fixture has a HEAD-bound proof pointer.

RED-STRATEGY DECIDED (2026-06-21, Akien) — see D-proof-on-close-2026-06-20.md 'Red-strategy + clean-tree principle RESOLVED'. (1) NO STASH (hides state) and NO worktree (editable-finder shadowing bug). Red = in-place 'git checkout <baseline> -- <impl-paths>' (impl-paths = HEAD~1..HEAD diff minus the test file), run, restore via 'git checkout HEAD -- <impl-paths>' — safe BECAUSE clean tree is required. Works WITH the PEP 660 editable finder. (2) No-hidden-state principle: uncommitted work = deferred/hidden state (git's hollow build); must be committed or reverted immediately, never stashed. (3) HALT until sorted: dirty tree halts work at (a) start of a unit of work and (b) proof/close time; workflow-halt (CC stops+sorts), NOT bus-HALT; scope = boundaries+proof-time, not continuous. (4) Target modes: WORKING = current local HEAD pushed-or-not (default daily driver); BRANCH <name> = named tip. OPEN: HEAD=origin/pushed distinct mode vs synonym for WORKING; BRANCH baseline merge-base vs tip~1. The clean-tree guard in proof_emitter.py is the proof-time half, already shipped.

SIMPLIFICATION (2026-06-21, Akien): drop the WORKING/HEAD/BRANCH/origin mode trio — premature. NO MODES. Proof runs against the current committed HEAD; red = HEAD~1. Discipline: commit when you think it's right (before testing + each bug-fix cycle); push when certain. Push state is not a harness concern. The no-stash + halt-until-sorted + in-place-checkout-red parts all stand.

FLOW CONSEQUENCE (advisor, note for build): a successful prove() writes the proof JSON to devlab/runtime/memory/proofs/ INSIDE the repo (untracked). The clean-tree precondition uses full --porcelain (with untracked), so the NEXT prove()/work-start halts until that proof is committed. Arguably correct ('commit the proof, then proceed') and consistent with no-hidden-state — but the close-gate flow must commit the emitted proof as part of close, or sequencing will halt. Tests don't catch this (isolated_store puts proofs outside the repo).

SPEC FINALIZED (2026-06-21, Akien) — see D-proof-on-close-2026-06-20.md 'Close-gate RESOLVED + CP1-6 review'. (1) Proof required to close: proof with links.tickets ∋ ticket; VALID = proof.commit reachable from HEAD AND no drift in recorded impl_paths (git diff proof.commit HEAD -- <impl_paths> empty). PREREQ: emitter must record impl_paths in the proof body (already computed in _git_inplace_red). (2) NO load-bearing discriminator — DROPPED as an escape hatch. Every ticket proves OR closes shipped-unproven = flag + mandatory reason that NAMES THE MISSING PROOF-LEVER (visible backlog; CP1 honest admission). (3) Conceptual tickets stay a deliberate nuisance until we find the lever. (4) Bootstrap (emitter/close-gate/evaluator) closes shipped-unproven 'can't self-prove'. (5) Gate in cc_queue.py status->closed path. shipped-unproven needs no approval yet; watch for abuse. CLAUDE.md updated: CP1-6 thin-shim + revised 'every ticket proves or declares unproven' rule (supersedes load-bearing rule).
body.result: Proof-on-close gate BUILT + PROVEN LIVE (commit 1ffb7f13). Single _proof_gate chokepoint wired into ALL status->closed paths (cmd_close + cmd_setstatus(closed) + _try_auto_validate) — verified no bypass. proof_store (find_for_ticket on bare body.id + git-reachability + impl-path-scoped drift). proof_emitter records impl_paths. effective_status renders shipped-unproven distinctly. 31 tests green; LIVE smoke under system python3: list imports clean + bare proofless close REFUSED with ticket unmutated. NO load-bearing discriminator (dropped). BOOTSTRAP: this piece can't self-prove -> close-state is shipped-unproven 'can't self-prove' on Akien's nod (dogfood: gate's first real use = closing itself unproven). FIRST-EXERCISE: git-drift validity path unexercised live until a real proof exists in store (same as emitter). SYSTEM-WIDE: bare proofless closes reject; auto-validate+sprint-ticket default shipped-unproven w/ named lever.
